title: Logistics Team Member
description: **Role Title:** Logistics Team Member **Location:** Vhi Dublin **Grade:** Senior Admin **Contract:** 9 month fixed term contract Vhi Health \& Wellbeing DAC currently has a 9 month fixed term contract role for the position of Logistics Team Member This role will contribute to the operations of the logistics area in line with company policy. To ensure excellent logistic support to the Vhi Hospital@Home clinical team and the Corporate LiveWell team **About Us** Our purpose at Vhi is to help our customers and patients live longer, stronger and healthier lives. A big part of delivering this purpose is to create a workplace culture where all colleagues feel connected, informed and motivated to do their best work. At Vhi, we have a set of shared values that drive the kind of behaviour we want and that will support colleagues to grow, develop and play a key part in our success. Leading the way in private healthcare, we now offer an extensive range of healthcare services, including: urgent care, planned care, primary care, health screening, hospital @ home, women’s health, digital health, out of hours emergency dental care, and nurseline 24/7\. The efficient running of these services is made possible by our teams of highly skilled healthcare professionals. **What We Offer** In addition to offering a competitive basic salary, the offer comes with the following benefits: * Bonus * Health Insurance * Pension * Income Continuance * Medical indemnity * EAP In addition, Vhi offers a range of services and programs designed to support your physical, mental, and emotional health. Services include the Vhi 360 health centre with lifestyle and complementary therapies, an **What Will You Do** Stores area responsibilities * Liaise with the Logistics Team Lead to ensure smooth running of the logistics area and ensure company policy is adhered to particularly in relation to infection control and Dangerous Goods Safety Advisor (DSGA) policy. * Order and rotate approved stock as requested by the Logistics Team Lead. * Fulfil daily orders from the Clinical Staff for consumables and liaise with the clinical staff regarding collections and delivery of stock. * Liaise with pharmacy daily to deliver prescriptions and collect supplies as requested. * Liaise with Vhi Hospital@Home patients and their relatives in a professional manner to arrange delivery of stock to their homes. * Assist patients with placement of stock in the home. * Report back to Logistics Team Lead any issues regarding stock and be proactive in ensuring there is sufficient stock in the store taking lead\-times into consideration. * Monitor movement of medical electronic equipment to patient homes, assist with weekly swap\-outs and assist with weekly quality control (QC) of equipment. * Ensure all returned medical equipment is decontaminated as per infection control policy, labelled and stored as per company policy. * Ensure all chemicals are managed safely as per DGSA policy. * Ensure all Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (ADR) documentation is completed daily and submitted in line with company policy. * Ensure all Infection Control (IPC) policies are adhered to and carry out monthly self\-audit of logistics area in conjunction with IPC Nurse and Logistics Team Lead. * Ensure on a daily basis the logistic area is temperature controlled and report any variances to the Logistics Team Lead. * Liaise with clinical staff regarding collection of clinical waste, ensure transportation is within DGSA guidelines and ensure the clinical waste room is maintained in line with company/DGSA policy. * Always ensure for patients receiving medication an anaphylaxis kit is included with the supplies and tracker is completed. * Report any issues to the Logistics Team Lead. * Assist the Logistics Team Lead with the annual calibration programme by rotating equipment to staff and entering calibration updates on the Q pulse system. * Be flexible in times of emergency or adverse weather conditions where extended working hours will be required. Blood Collection Responsibilities * Co\-ordinate all blood collections with nursing and medical staff to ensure specimens are collected and transported to the lab on time and in line with DGSA policy. * Plan an efficient route daily to ensure the urgent bloods are collected earlier in the day. * Liaise with the laboratory daily to ensure all samples are checked in and receipts returned. * Send a daily report to Vhi Hospital@Home doctors regarding which bloods were sent to the laboratory. * Ensure all documentation is completed prior to and when the blood arrives in the laboratory. * Ensure there is a supply of blood collection equipment and forms available for staff and stores area. * Record all non\-conformances with the laboratory on Q Pulse. Doctor Transport * Safely transport doctors and medical supplies to and from patient homes using the most time efficient route. * Identify locations to be visited and develop an effective route plan to meet the daily journey requirements. * Advise doctors of efficient routes based on work plan for the day to maximise time and reduce fuel consumption. * Report to Logistics Team Lead if medical staff are using inefficient routes to see patients. * Liaise with patients in advance of doctor visit to agree a suitable arrival time for the doctor. * Ensure the vehicle is in good working order and daily vehicle checklist has been completed * Ensure there is and spill kit in the vehicle and be familiar with its indications and use. * Ensure the emergency equipment and doctor’s equipment is checked and ready for use. * Transport doctor’s emergency and drug bag from the Safe to car daily. * Check expiry dates of consumables as part of daily emergency checklist. * Report any non\-conformances to the logistics Team Lead. * Ensure medical drug bag is removed from the vehicle daily and locked away in the Safe. * Assist the doctor in the event of a cardiac arrest of patient or other emergencies as outlined in Basic Life Support (BLS) training. * Complete and submit weekly medical utilisation reports. Corporate LiveWell Logistics * Work with the Logistics Team Lead when assigned to the Corporate LiveWell team to ensure there is efficient delivery and collection of all supplies to the corporate site. * Liaise with the dedicated HR person in the corporate site to gain access to the LiveWell rooms for delivery and collections. * Work with the on\-line LiveWell planner to ensure correct stock ready for each site. * Assist with the monthly Quality Control (QC) of the point of care equipment. * Rotate cholesterol equipment into Harcourt Street for monthly QC in conjunction with the screening lead nurse. * Undertake such other duties as may be assigned from time to time by the Logistics Team Lead or Operations Manager. **What you need to be successful Education \& Experience** * Full clean driving licence. * Previous experience working in a logistics department. * An aptitude for or previous experience in a medical or pharmaceutical environment is desirable * Good analytical skills. * Excellent IT skills. **Interpersonal Skills** * Good written and oral communication skills * Prioritising and organisational skills are essential. * Customer focused with caring and helpful attitude is essential. * Demonstrated alignment to Vhi values **Sustainability** At Vhi, we believe that the health of people and the health of the planet are interconnected. That’s why we’re on a journey towards an integrated model of healthcare that puts people and the planet at its heart. **Our Commitment to Inclusion and Accessibility** At Vhi, we are on a continuous journey to build an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and respected. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and perspectives We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations at every stage of the recruitment process for candidates with disabilities, neurodivergence, or medical conditions. If you require an accommodation, please contact recruitment@vhi.ie, and a member of our recruitment team will be in touch via email. Any information you provide will be treated confidentially within the recruitment team and used solely for the purpose of determining and providing appropriate accommodations during the application and recruitment process. **Vhi is an equal opportunities employer** **Ready to start your journey? Apply now.**

title: Looking to Progress Your Career?
description: * **Unique Leadership Academy – Our Leaders Grow Internally** * **Take your Career to the Next Level – Financial \& Career Progression** * **Carve your path and boost your career in a meritocratic organisation.** Looking to advance your career, learn new skills, and make a positive impact? When you join us as an **Associate Recruitment Consultant** in Dublin you have the opportunity to use your existing skills and be supported to develop a rewarding career with outstanding growing potential. You will connect great talent with meaningful employment and offer top\-quality consultancy to our clients. **We believe in continuous professional development for all of our team members and therefore offer access to a training program to become a Certified Recruitment Consultant.** Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, which come attached with uncapped earning potential. **Key Responsibilities:** * Collaborating with clients to understand their hiring needs and develop tailored recruitment strategies. * Sourcing, screening, and presenting qualified candidates to match client requirements. * Build and maintain lasting relationships with candidates, guiding them through the recruitment process. * Managing candidate portfolios, ensuring database management, compliance, and reference checks. **What We Look for in Our Teammates:** * 1 to 3 years of professional experience in any field (such as Events, Sales, Customer Service, Banking, Retail, Insurance, etc…). * Comfortable working toward targets and KPIs * Available to work on\-site in Dublin 2 (Monday–Friday) **Salary \& Benefits We Offer:** * Continuous learning \& professional development \- renowned training programme plus Leadership Academy * Clear progression structure with set goals to achieve for each promotion – a true meritocracy * Flexible benefits package built by you * Dublin city location * Quarterly team events \+ wellness activities \+ Friday drinks For more information, contact Nadia Garcia at \+353 1 572 1159 or nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Ballybrack (A96 CD72, D18 F5F7\) **Hours:** 5\.5 hours per week **Days:** Monday \& Friday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for this flexible and customer focused role where every day offers fresh challenges; from recognising new sales/merchandising opportunities, to displaying brand new ranges. As a part time Greeting Card Merchandiser you will be the face of Carlton Greetings, calling upon retail outlets in and around your local area, ensuring our Customers’ displays are of the highest standard. **The role involves:** * Visiting local retailers, high street chains or supermarkets * Tidying \& filling greeting card \& gift dressing displays * Locating \& unpacking deliveries for replenishment of displays * Installation of new displays * No cold calling or direct selling is involved **To be a Part Time Merchandiser, you will need to have:** * Reliable \& conscientious work ethic * Flexibility around working times \& notably during peak seasonal periods when overtime is likely to be required * The initiative \& ability to work on your own * A strong team ethic * A confident \& friendly personality * No experience is required, as we offer full training * Full driving licence (desirable) So if you love to get out and about, enjoy meeting new people and would like to be part of a great team that promotes and values diversity, then this may be the role for you! WHO WE ARE At Carlton Greetings we are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We help make the world a more thoughtful and caring place every single day with our greeting cards, gift dressings and social expressions products created for life’s big events, special occasions and those “saw\-this\-and\-thought\-of\-you” moments of life. Along with our sister Company John Sands, we’re part of the American Greetings Corporation, one of the largest privately owned greeting card publishers in the world. We’re one of the largest direct to retail publisher of greeting cards and social expression products in the UK \- so you’ll find our amazing products just about everywhere! **Here at Carlton Greetings we take pride in being an inclusive and diverse employer \- we** **are dedicated and committed to building a culture where difference is valued and** **everyone is able to** **fulfil** **their potential at work regardless of their background.**

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Managing Consultant
description: *At Fruition Group Ireland we embrace our values of* ***Pride*** *,* ***Energy*** *and* ***Perseverance*** *and our colleagues embed them as the foundation of their work. Our colleagues are proud to work for Fruition Group, they are pro\-active with high energy to maintain our market\-leading status. This is a great opportunity to join a team on a* ***Managing*** *level with possibilities to lead and be part of a team with both current and new colleagues!* ***The role*** Reporting directly into the Directors, who are some of Irelands biggest billers. You will play a pivotal part in the resourcing of candidates for our clients and the development of new client relationships. You will continue to develop your network of candidates within the tech (perm and contract) space and continually keeping up to date with current trends. well organised * Proactive and independent thinker * Excellent customer service skills * Strong presentation skills with the ability to convey key messages to diverse and wide audiences * Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement skills * Experience working to targets * Great negotiating skills * Excellent Experience using the telephone in a professional environment is desirable * Great understanding of technical recruitment * Experience within a sales environment is desirable ***Your Benefits to name a few:*** * **Competitive basic salary with uncapped earning potential** * **Lucrative commission – no thresholds!** * **Ongoing training with external learning providers** * **Clear and structured Career progression** * **Biannual winners trip to European destinations such as Marbella, Barcelona \& Amsterdam** * **Flexible working offer \- between the office and home** * **Quarterly lunch clubs for top achievers** * **Quarterly team socials** * **Friday early finish** * **Private healthcare** * **Pension Contribution** If you’re interested in joining Fruition as Managing Consultant, please get in touch for an initial confidential chat about our available opportunities, and we’ll happily share our story with you. For further information please email lois.gooding@fruitiongroup.com We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age.

Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings \[NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. title: Mathematics Expert (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Mathematics Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your expertise in advanced mathematics could directly shape how AI reasons, solves problems, and communicates with the world? We're looking for Mathematics experts — Masters and PhD holders — to help build and refine the mathematical reasoning capabilities of cutting\-edge AI models. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. You'll work asynchronously alongside world\-class AI researchers, applying your deep mathematical knowledge to problems that genuinely matter. No prior AI experience needed — your expertise in mathematics is what counts. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Develop, solve, and critically review advanced mathematical problems with real\-world relevance * Design complex, rigorous problem statements across algebra, calculus, statistics, discrete mathematics, or related areas * Evaluate AI\-generated mathematical reasoning and outputs for correctness, clarity, and depth * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and domain experts to strengthen AI model performance * Ensure the highest standards of mathematical rigor and precision across all deliverables * Work independently on task\-based assignments at your own pace and schedule **Who You Are** * Holds a Master's or PhD in Mathematics or a closely related field * Deep expertise in one or more of: algebra, calculus, statistics, or discrete mathematics * Comfortable coding in Python, MATLAB, or Mathematica for research or applied problem\-solving * Exceptionally detail\-oriented — you catch errors others miss and care deeply about precision * Clear and confident written communicator, able to explain complex ideas with rigor and clarity * Fluent in English and currently based in the U.S., Canada, U.K., Australia, or New Zealand **Nice to Have** * Prior experience in data annotation, evaluation systems, or AI quality assurance * Background in mathematical research, academic publishing, or technical writing * Familiarity with AI tools or machine learning concepts * Experience designing or grading advanced problem sets in an academic setting **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs and AI teams * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, intellectually stimulating work * Apply your advanced expertise to problems at the frontier of AI development * Make a direct, tangible impact on how AI understands and reasons through mathematics * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Mid Full Stack Developer (Python / React / AWS)
description: **Full Stack Developer (Python / React / AWS)** Salary: €60,000 to €80,000 DOE \+ bonus \+ benefits Location: Dublin City Centre Hybrid: 3 days per week in the office Our client is a global financial services company supporting investment funds, multinational companies and private clients worldwide. With over 6,000 employees across more than 40 countries, they build technology platforms that support key financial operations such as treasury, collateral management and trade processing. Dublin is one of their main technology hubs where many strategic systems are developed. Our client is looking for a Full Stack Developer to join a global engineering team building core platforms used by treasury, collateral and trade operations teams. You will work across the full development lifecycle building modern cloud based systems. The stack is focused on React on the frontend and Python on the backend running on AWS. There is currently a strong focus on improving and redesigning user interfaces across multiple applications. **Must Have (Key Requirements)** * 2\+ years Python development experience * AWS cloud experience * Experience using code generation tools such as GitHub Copilot or Amazon Q * Experience with a JavaScript framework such as React or Angular * Experience building or integrating APIs * Strong understanding of system design and modern development practices * The main stack includes Python, React, FastAPI, PostgreSQL and AWS. Experience with Django or Flask is also relevant. **Nice to Have** * DevOps experience * Cloud native architecture experience * Understanding of GenAI or agentic systems * Event driven architecture * Financial services experience **What You’ll Do** * Build new features and improve existing platforms * Develop frontend interfaces and backend services * Design and integrate APIs * Work with AWS cloud services and databases * Write unit tests and support test driven development The hiring manager is looking for **strong engineers who enjoy building systems and working across both frontend and backend** in a collaborative team environment.

title: Mobile Mechanic

In this growth phase, the role is critical. Done well, it builds trust, protects vehicle uptime, and ensures the right work flows back into the workshop properly. **Key Responsibilities** * Attend mobile jobs **on time** and complete in\-scope work safely and efficiently * Carry out repairs neatly and to a **high standard** * Capture clear **proof of work**, including photos and notes where required * Maintain **van cleanliness**, stock awareness, and proper equipment care * Communicate clearly with the **Senior Mobile Technician and front desk** when jobs change, fail, or need escalation * Represent United Tyres Autoservice professionally with **customers, drivers, and fleet contacts** **What Success Looks Like** * Strong punctuality and **safe, consistent workmanship** * Clear and professional **job completion and handover discipline** * Smooth hand\-offs when jobs require **workshop support, recovery, or parts** * A well\-maintained van and **reliable field performance** **What We’re Looking For** * Confident carrying out **on\-site repairs** (brakes, discs, batteries, punctures, basic mechanical work) * Good judgment \- knows when a roadside fix should **not be pushed too far** * Strong work ethic, **reliability, and attendance** * Professional attitude with a **calm and customer\-focused approach** Apply via Indeed or contact us directly at: 0872183327 \- Adrian unitedtyreswalkinstown@gmail.com Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * Motor Mechanic: 3 years (required) Language: * English (preferred) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Multiple Openings (Associate), Fund/Client Accounting
description: **We welcome you to apply!** When applying to this general posting, our expert BNY Talent Acquisition Team may also review your resume for consideration across other open roles within the company. At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day around the globe, our 50,000\&\#43; employees bring the power of their perspective to the table to create solutions with our clients that benefit businesses, communities and people everywhere. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we help make money work for the world – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team members for the role of **Fund Accountants (Associate)** to join our **Fund Accounting team** . This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Perform accurate and timely delivery of daily NAV calculations in a fast paced and rapidly evolving environment and act as point of escalation for the team for senior / team lead positions * Maintain open lines of communication with key internal teams to ensure accurate delivery of NAV critical inputs, in line with internal service delivery agreements * Critically analyse fund movement and act as point of contact for queries or escalation items from the team within the NAV production environment * Investigate and ensure full close out of operational and audit queries, from both internal and external stakeholders * Manage daily operational requirements across the team, including but not limited to team allocation of duties, annual leave, team performance etc. * Provide key considerations and feedback for initiatives to support change and improvements in alignment with the firm's strategy * Ensure escalation of items requiring urgent resolution to Management Team in a timely manner To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * 3\-7\&\#43; years’ Fund Accounting experience within Operations * Prior experience using Enterprise Invest One is highly preferred * Knowledge of the components of daily NAV calculations and complex financial instruments * Knowledge of Fund Accounting requirements for UK OEICs and/or Luxembourg SICAVs an advantage * Skilled in critically analysing all aspects of NAV Production to identify items requiring further investigation or escalation for senior level positions * Third level degree qualified, ideally in Accounting or Business * Excellent communication skills At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2024 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune, 2024 Human Rights Campaign Foundation, Corporate Equality Index, 100% score, 2023\-2024 Best Places to Work for Disability Inclusion, Disability: IN – 100% score, 2023\-2024 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2024 Dow Jones Sustainability Indices, Top performing company for Sustainability, 2024 Bloomberg’s Gender Equality Index (GEI), 2023 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans. This is a talent pipeline posting and does not represent any one particular job opening. By applying to this pipeline requisition, your interest will be reviewed for multiple potential openings based upon your background and disclosed work preference.

title: Named Account Executive
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Sales Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. The biggest banks and insurance companies in Poland are under pressure to modernise — and they need a trusted partner to help them do it. That's where you come in. At Salesforce, we're not looking for someone to manage accounts. We're looking for someone to *transform* them. As a Strategic Enterprise Account Executive for Financial Services in Poland, you'll sit at the heart of some of the most consequential technology decisions in the market — shaping 3–5 year transformation agendas for Tier 1 and Tier 2 financial institutions, and building the kind of executive relationships that last. This is a role for people who are energised by complexity, not intimidated by it. **What You'll Be Doing** * Owning a portfolio of Poland's top banking and insurance institutions — not managing it, *owning* it * Building and executing account strategies that go way beyond pipeline hygiene — think multi\-year transformation roadmaps * Leading high\-value, multi\-stakeholder sales cycles with CIOs, CTOs, COOs, and Heads of Digital, Risk, and Distribution * Positioning Salesforce's full platform — Sales, Service, Marketing, Data, and AI — as the answer to real business problems: growth, cost efficiency, regulatory pressure, and customer experience * Orchestrating world\-class internal teams (Solution Engineering, Customer Success, Industry Advisors, Partners) to win big * Landing, expanding, and growing accounts at scale — with pipeline that's real, qualified, and predictable **What You Bring** * 10\+ years in enterprise software or SaaS sales — with a track record of 7\-figure\+ deals * Proven experience selling into banking and/or insurance in Poland *(this one's non\-negotiable)* * Confidence operating at C\-level — you challenge thinking, reframe problems, and hold your ground * A deep understanding of what keeps financial institutions up at night: legacy transformation, regulation, digital channels, cost pressure * High ownership mindset — you don't wait for resources, you pull them in * Fluency in Polish and English **Even better if you have:** * Experience with platform sales (CRM, data, cloud, AI) * An existing network within Polish financial institutions * The ability to spot an opportunity inside an RFP — not just respond to one **What Success Looks Like Here** You're consistently delivering against multi\-million quotas. You're the trusted advisor your clients call before they call anyone else. You're expanding accounts from a single use case into enterprise\-wide adoption. And your forecast? It's one you're proud to stand behind. **Why Salesforce** We're the global \#1 in CRM, Data, and AI — and in Poland's financial services market, our brand opens doors. You'll have access to the broadest platform in the industry, the credibility to walk into any boardroom, and the genuine opportunity to shape large\-scale digital transformation in one of Central Europe's most dynamic markets. We're also a company that believes business is the greatest platform for change — and that the best work happens when everyone feels seen, valued, and empowered to bring their whole self to work. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: National Account Executive - Dublin - 13-15 Month FTC
description: **Who we are?** We are JDE Peet’s. We craft coffee for every cup and build brands for every heart. JDE Peet’s is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, serving approximately 4,400 cups of coffee per second in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focusing on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and Jacobs, alongside a collection of 9 local icons; such as Douwe Egberts, Kenco, Pilao, Old Town, Moccona, Super, Gevalia, Friele, Marcilla. In 2024, JDE Peet’s generated total sales of EUR 8\.8 billion and employed a global workforce of more than 21,000 employees. **What’s it like to work at JDE Peet’s?** We believe amazing things happen over a cup of coffee. Together, we create those moments every day, turning ideas into impact and possibilities into reality. At JDE Peet’s, we work as one team guided by our values: we Dare to Amaze, Own It, Make It Simple, and Win Together. Here, you’ll find the freedom to innovate, the courage to take bold steps, and the support to grow your career in a global environment. Come join our team and discover what your cup of amazing can be! Find out more at http://careers.jdepeets.com **Job Description** The National Account Executive supports the management and growth of key national customers across Ireland. The role focuses on supporting Account Managers, executing customer plans, analysing sales data, and ensuring excellence in promotional admin execution. This role is an incredible opportunity for anyone who has an interest in the FMCG industry. The successful candidate will have the chance to develop, improve, and hone highly transferable commercial skills applicable across industries and markets. Additional to the National Account Executive responsibilities, our candidate will have the opportunity to assist marketing and category management, gaining firsthand knowledge of these exciting roles. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the National Account managers with their day\-today tasks, including new product set up, running reports, creating presentations etc. * Prepare and submit promotional submissions in line with the customer calendars. * Create promotional briefs to share with the field sales team. * Manage promotional funding and forecasting in our internal platform XTEL. * Monitor customer pricing and amend any discrepancies in SAP. * Support Marketing with PR activity and budget management. * Assist Category Management with data analysis and creating presentations. **Qualifications** Working at JDE Peet’s is hands\-on, giving you the chance to leave your mark while working together as one team. We’re a fast\-paced company on a mission, focused on what matters most. Our environment demands energy, agility, and bold thinking. If you’re ready to make an impact and grow in a global team, JDE Peet’s is the place for you. For this role as a National Account Executive, we’re looking for someone who has the following skills and experience: * 1\-3 years’ experience in sales or a commercial role (FMCG experience highly valued). * Proficient with Microsoft Office programmes, particularly Excel. * Strong numerical and analytical skills with high attention to detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced commercial environment. * Full right to work/work permits for the duration of the contract **Additional Information** **Your application at JDE Peet's** If you are interested in this career opportunity at JDE Peet's, apply and join us. After receiving your application we will do our utmost to give you quick feedback. Part of the recruitment procedure at JDE Peet's would be an online assessment, followed by interviews. A pre\-employment check can be part of the recruitment process. **Our DE\&I Statement** At JDE Peet’s we aim to create a better future where we authentically serve, reflect and embrace everyone. We believe in fostering a more diverse, equitable and inclusive organisation where everyone feels comfortable to truly be who they are and unleash their full potential. This means we do not tolerate discrimination on the basis of our race, ethnicity, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, age, disability, or any other trait protected by law. Throughout the entire employee lifecycle, we place DE\&I at the core of our people processes. This enables us to drive objectivity and eliminate any potential bias, ensuring an inclusive experience in the workplace. Do not hesitate to reach out if you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. **Our efforts on this agenda have earned us the prestigious Catalyst Award, as well as the Fair Pay Leader Certification in 2025 thanks to an unexplained gender pay gap of 0\.52%.** *\#unleashyourpossibility \#acupofamazing \#li\-hybrid* JDE Peet’s \- now part of Keurig Dr Pepper – is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, with a presence in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focused on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and our 10 strategically selected local icons led by Jacobs.

title: Network Engineer
description: About Us Diacom is a rapidly growing Managed IT Service Provider, delivering enterprise\-grade IT solutions across networking, managed IT services, and voice telecommunications. Our tech stack includes HPE Aruba \& Fortinet networking, Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Azure, and Microsoft 365, and an industry\-leading cybersecurity platform in the MSP IT space. Our growth means we need specialists who can maintain the rapid response times our clients depend on. You’ll be joining a team that values curiosity, has autonomy, and demands technical excellence. The Role The Networks Engineer (L2\) supports the delivery of Diacom’s networking projects, solutions, and services across new and existing customer environments. The role is responsible for the configuration, deployment, maintenance, and troubleshooting of network infrastructure, while ensuring continuity of service, customer satisfaction, and ongoing revenue for the networks vertical of the business. The position also contributes to the development of new service lines, supports the helpdesk with escalations, and maintains high\-quality documentation and technical standards. What You'll Do • Lead the design and deployment of enterprise\-grade network solutions for new and existing customer environments. • Provide expert guidance on network strategy, capacity planning, segmentation, scalability, and security design. • Oversee the maintenance, performance, and reliability of customer network infrastructure, including firewalls, switches, wireless access points, and supporting systems. • Ensure best\-practice configuration, deployment, and lifecycle management of: o Switching platforms (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Wireless solutions (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Firewalls (Fortinet/FortiGate, Palo Alto) o NAC/AAA platforms (Aruba ClearPass) • Plan, coordinate, and execute scheduled firmware upgrades, maintenance windows, and infrastructure refresh activities. • Act as the senior escalation point for complex and high\-impact network incidents from the Diacom Helpdesk and TAC teams. • Lead root\-cause analysis (RCA), remediation planning, and implementation of long\-term resolution strategies. • Produce detailed reports for internal and external stakeholders covering network health, performance trends, and incident outcomes. • Collaborate with VoIP, Managed Services, and Cybersecurity teams on cross\-functional projects and integrated solutions. • Provide technical input and subject\-matter expertise to sales and account management teams, including solution design and pre\-sales activities. • Provide technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge\-sharing to junior network engineers. • Contribute to internal technical training, standards development, and process improvement initiatives. • Participate in the after\-hours support roster for critical infrastructure and customer\-impacting events. • Provide availability for overtime during major project deployments or urgent customer requirements as required. What We're Looking For • Exposure to/or experience with enterprise networking technologies (Aruba, Fortinet, Palo Alto). • Full Driver’s License • Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills across wired and wireless networks. • Understanding of NAC principles and AAA frameworks. • Familiarity with network monitoring tools. • Strong communication and customer service orientation. • Ability to produce accurate technical documentation. Nice to Have • Basic understanding of networking concepts, operating systems, and common security controls • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills • Ability to work effectively in a team environment and follow established procedures

title: Network Engineer
description: Want Your Next Big Opportunity as a Network Engineer? I’m Here to Help! **About Your New Employer** * A forward\-thinking leader in technology and consulting services, recognised for its commitment to innovation and employee development. * Be part of a dynamic IT team managing cutting\-edge infrastructure in a global, fast\-paced environment. * Enjoy a supportive culture that values proactive problem\-solving, operational excellence, and personal growth. **What Skills You Need In This Job As A Network Engineer:** * In\-depth experience with Cisco, Nexus, and Brocade networking equipment. * Strong understanding of OSPF, BGP, Layer 2, and Layer 3 protocols. * Proficient with Cisco wireless LAN controller management and troubleshooting. * Solid grasp of network operating systems (JUNOS, Cisco IOS). * Excellent analytical and troubleshooting skills. * Capable of working independently with self\-sustaining motivation. * Strong organisational and mentoring abilities. **About Your New Job As A Network Engineer:** * Independently configure, troubleshoot, and maintain LAN, WAN, Wireless networks, and traffic engineering devices. * Manage, configure, and troubleshoot Cisco Nexus (9K, 7K, 5K, 2K), stack switches, and Brocade devices. * Handle EIGRP and BGP routing, as well as OSPF, and maintain network documentation and SOPs. * Standardise access rules across the environment and implement firewall best practices. * Oversee wireless LAN controllers, access points, and facilitate device refresh projects with a focus on security and manageability. * Address shipping, logistics, and provide user communication/support at local sites. * Proactively monitor infrastructure, anticipate potential issues, and recommend improvements for network performance, capacity, and scalability. **What’s On Offer In This Job As A Network Engineer:** * €500 Daily Rate. * 3 Month Rolling Contract with High Likelihood of Extensions * 4 Day Work Week What’s not to like?! I’m looking forward to chatting with some great Network Engineers.

title: New Product Development Executive
description: LED Group is a global lighting company built and sustained by talented people, who deliver innovation and new practices that drive our growth strategy. We are a business built on customer loyalty. We understand the value of trust, it's at the heart of everything we do. Our HQ is located in Dublin, with distribution and offices Globally. We currently have over 3,000 stockists and tens of thousands of installers and employ over 250 talented people around the world. ***Overview \- Lead end\-to\-end new product development, coordinating global teams, managing data and processes, and ensuring successful, on\-time product launches.*** **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** * Lead and manage the full lifecycle of new product development projects, from concept through to commercial launch. * Management of product samples, ordering, delivering, reviewing, cataloguing. * Coordinate cross\-functional teams including Quality Control, Technical, Sales, Marketing and Supply Chain. * Daily communication with colleagues in China to update and follow up actions. * Monitor and record all project activity through updating the main New Product * Development databases and individual Project workbooks. * Management and review of product technical data ensuring accuracy and consistency. * Review new product designs with Product Designers, collate and provide feedback. * Identify and manage project risks, challenges and mitigation strategies to ensure timely delivery. * Complete comparison sheets and make suggestions based on evaluation of data. * Drive continuous improvement in product development processes and internal project management practices. * Collaborate with Sales and Marketing departments in producing catalogues and promotional materials to support launches of ROBUS products worldwide * Present project updates, timelines and recommendations to senior management and key stakeholders. * Some travel is required – visiting our customers in different markets, attending lighting industry trade shows and exhibitions, visiting our partner manufacturers in Asia. **Key Requirements:** * Experience coordinating cross\-functional teams across departments, regions, and timezones. * Ability to work to own initiative and taking full responsibility for a project. * Excellent planning and organisation skills. Very strong attention to detail. * Ambitious multi\-tasker, ability to work on multiple projects at the same time. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools including Excel, Outlook and Word. * Good people skills with proven ability to develop relationships throughout the company and supplier base. * Demonstrated initiative, problem\-solving ability and attention to detail. * Demonstrates strong ability to interpret and communicate technical information. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively across different cultures and time zones. * Strong organisational and project management skills. * Fluent English, spoken and written.

title: Newly Qualified Accountant
description: **NEWLY QUALIFIED ACCOUNTANT OPPORTUNITY** \- Balbriggan, County Dublin We are seeking a Newly Qualified Accountant (ACA / ACCA or equivalent) who is ready to step into a client\-facing role with real responsibility from day one. This is an opportunity to move beyond training and take ownership of a diverse client portfolio within a dynamic and supportive environment. Role purpose You will support the client engagement team in delivering high\-quality accounting and advisory services while managing your own portfolio of clients. The role combines technical accounting, client relationship management, and exposure to advisory work. **Key responsibilities** * Take ownership of a client portfolio, including year\-end accounts, management accounts, budgets and forecasts for selected clients * Ensure all client deadlines and deliverables are met, working efficiently and to agreed budgets * Manage and develop client relationships, ensuring high levels of satisfaction and identifying opportunities to provide additional support * Deliver end\-to\-end accounting work, from initial preparation through to final accounts sign\-off * Oversee the completion of accounts and ensure all client queries are dealt with promptly and professionally * Support and deliver specialist advisory and project work, providing clear analysis, options and recommendations * Maintain up\-to\-date technical knowledge, including relevant legislation and accounting standards * Assist with tenders and proposals for new business opportunities * Support the development and training of junior team members * Work effectively with cloud accounting systems, including Xero, and other digital tools **Requirements** * Recently qualified ACA, ACCA or equivalent professional qualification * Previous experience in a similar accounting or client\-facing role * Experience managing or supporting a client portfolio * Strong knowledge of GAAP and accounting standards, particularly FRS 102 1A * Experience in technical compliance and advisory work * Familiarity with cloud accounting software such as Xero * Experience in mentoring or training junior staff * Strong communication skills and a proactive, solutions\-focused approach This role offers the opportunity to take full ownership of client work, develop strong advisory skills, and progress in a modern, technology\-driven accounting environment where responsibility and client impact are central to the role.

title: OTC Sales Assistant
description: OTC Sales Assistant \- Artane Castle SC, Dublin 5 **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It’s the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don’t just embrace our differences – we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are – and that’s something we are really proud of. Which is why we’ve summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. We currently have an exciting opportunity for a part time OTC Sales Assistant in our Charlesland Pharmacy. ***It isn’t just any shop floor, it’s the heart of the community.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us** 💼 We’ve got your back: employee assistance programme 🛍️ Perks you’ll actually use: Generous in store discounts 🌈 Belonging at Work: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you’re valued for *you* . 👶 Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Drive Sales: increase O.T.C sales through link selling. * Put Patients First: advise and assist customers with all store products. * Deliver Exceptional Patient Care: engage actively with customers and provide a high standard in customer service. * Stay Fully Informed: develop excellent product knowledge. * Point of Sale Accuracy: Perform till duties with efficiency, accuracy, and attention to detail, **About McCabes Pharmacy** McCabes Pharmacy is one of Ireland's most loved Pharmacy providers, pioneering truly innovative services over the past 40 years. We are founded on values of compassionate care, advice you can trust, speedy service and excellent value for money. The patient and customer are truly at the heart of what we do. The teams that serve our customers are central to our organisation. Our commitment to our staff is to enable them to develop their careers and to have as many opportunities to be the best they can be. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland’s leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland’s leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe’s leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@mccabespharmacy.ie***

title: Part Time Admin Assistant
description: We are looking for a highly organised, proactive, and tech\-savvy Admin Assistant to support an award\-winning multi\-business owner and her manager across three growing businesses. This is a part\-time, in\-person role based between Dublin 7 and Dublin 2, working for approximately 10–20 hours per week for a 12\-month period. About the Role This role is ideal for someone who genuinely enjoys organisation, systems, and helping businesses run smoothly behind the scenes. You’ll be involved in a mix of structured administrative support and ad hoc tasks, helping improve day\-to\-day operations and overall efficiency across multiple businesses. Responsibilities * General administrative support across three businesses * Organising files, information, schedules, and workflows * Setting up and improving systems and operational processes * Supporting project coordination and day\-to\-day business tasks * Using tools such as Notion to manage information and workflows * Exploring and implementing AI tools and smart automations to improve efficiency * Assisting with various ad hoc admin tasks as needed We’re Looking For Someone Who: * Is highly organised and detail\-oriented * Enjoys creating systems and improving processes * Is comfortable learning and using technology and apps * Has experience with (or interest in) tools like Notion and AI platforms * Can work independently and take initiative * Is reliable, adaptable, and proactive * Has strong communication skills and a positive attitude Details * Part\-time: 10–20 hours per week * In\-person role based in Dublin 7 and Dublin 2 * Initial 12\-month commitment * Potential for longer\-term opportunity This is a great opportunity to work closely with a dynamic entrepreneur across multiple businesses and play an important role in helping operations run smoothly and efficiently.
