title: Summer Logistics & Transport Administrator (Part-Time)
description: **Great opportunity for students in Logistics / Supply Chain** **!!! Please note: this role is specifically suited to candidates studying or experienced in Logistics, Supply Chain or Transport Operations. This is NOT a general administration position. !!!** **About Us** Global Fruit Company Ltd. is a well\-established fresh produce supplier based in Dublin 11, operating within a fast\-paced international supply chain environment. We work closely with growers, suppliers, and transport partners to deliver fresh products efficiently across Ireland. **The Opportunity** We are looking for a motivated student or recent graduate to join our transport team for the summer. This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in a real logistics operation, working closely with a small, experienced team. You will be exposed to the day\-to\-day running of transport planning, supplier coordination, and administrative processes within a supply chain business. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Supporting transport operations with day\-to\-day administrative tasks * Processing and checking transport invoices * Updating collection schedules and transport plans * Preparing and organising transport documentation * Communicating with drivers and suppliers * Assisting the team during busy operational periods **What You’ll Gain** * Practical experience in transport and logistics operations * Exposure to real\-world supply chain processes * Opportunity to work closely with an experienced team * Insight into planning, coordination, and documentation in logistics * Potential for future opportunities within the business **What We’re Looking For** * Currently studying (or recently completed) logistics, supply chain, or a related field * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Comfortable using Excel and general office systems * Reliable, proactive, and willing to learn * Good communication skills **Working Pattern** 20–25 hours per week 4 days a week (including Friday – essential), 5\-6 h per day **Contract Duration** May to August 2026 Possibility to extend depending on performance and business needs **If you’re looking to gain valuable experience in logistics and supply chain this summer, we’d love to hear from you.** Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 4 months Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Expected hours: 20\.0 – 25\.0 per week Benefits: * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Supervisor/Trainee Supervisor (Full Time)
description: The role will be 30\-40 hours per week with some weekend work and the right candidate must be able to work flexibly throughout the week/weekend. **Responsibilities and Duties** The right candidate must: * Have the ability to work well under pressure * Must be trustworthy * Must be honest * Provide exceptional customer service * Have previous experience in retail * Hold the ability to motivate others * Have strong communication skills Duties include: * Liaising with the Management team to achieve best practice standards across the store * Adhering to health and safety procedures and standards * Ensuring the highest standards of customer service are provided * Opening the store as required * Closing the store as required * Communicating and managing staff as required Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Employee discount Application question(s): * Are you available for full time work all year round and not just during the summer months? Education: * Leaving Certificate (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Systems Administrator
description: **🚀 Elevate Your Career – Become Our Cyber Security IT Systems Administrator!** Are you ready to lead the charge in securing and scaling enterprise infrastructure? We’re looking for a **technical powerhouse** to drive security‑driven projects in a mission‑critical 24/7 environment. This is your chance to shape the future of IT operations while working at the cutting edge of Microsoft technologies. **What You’ll Do:** * Take the reins as **technical lead** on cyber security infrastructure initiatives. * Design, build, and harden complex Microsoft environments with resilience and scalability in mind. * Own **vulnerability management \& patching** to keep systems airtight. * Implement **high‑availability \& disaster recovery strategies** for critical applications. * Mentor junior staff and collaborate across teams to deliver secure, high‑performing solutions. * Drive continuous improvement with automation, monitoring, and failover planning. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years senior experience in Microsoft enterprise environments. * Certifications like **MCSA, MCP, CCNA** – plus **CompTIA Security\+** or Microsoft Security Associate. * Mastery of **Windows Server 2019/2022/2025** , **Active Directory \& Group Policy** , and network protocols. * Strong skills in backup solutions (NetBackup bonus), VMware, storage, and Linux (desirable). * Proven ability to solve problems, document processes, and thrive under ITIL‑driven methodologies. * Bonus points for automation chops with **Python, Ansible, REST APIs** . **Why Join Us?** You’ll be the **bridge between Cyber Security and IT Operations** , shaping infrastructure that meets business goals, regulatory expectations, and service‑level demands. This is more than a role—it’s a chance to **lead, innovate, and protect** at scale.

title: Systems Development Engineer, Edge Infrastructure Operations
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience with system automation and coding (e.g., Go, Python, etc.). * 2 years of experience with troubleshooting across Linux and networking. * 2 years of experience with Linux/Unix administration and internals. **Preferred qualifications:** * Understanding of internet peering/routing topologies (e.g., BGP), and front\-end development. * Excellent problem\-solving and communication skills. **About The Job** Systems Development Engineering (SDE) at Google is a role where you manage services and systems at scale. SDEs creatively put their engineering discipline to use automating the mundane and reducing toil. We don’t just write code to fix bugs, but emphasize the development of tools and solutions that fix classes of problems. We know it’s hard to control what you can’t measure – so we focus on observability: instrumenting first, then turning data into knowledge, and finally knowledge into action. We know that the operational efficiency of Google systems, services, virtual compute environments and the operating systems that power them impact the environment, not just the bottom line. We know that working together we can do more, and that community matters. Google brings together people with a wide variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We encourage them to collaborate, think big and take risks in a blame\-free environment. We promote self\-direction to work on meaningful projects, while we also strive to create an environment that provides the support and mentorship needed to learn and grow. Together we engineer and build the infrastructure, tools, access and telemetry for systems that enable orchestration of Google\-scale services. Come build things that matter. Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise\-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting\-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems. **Responsibilities** * Support Linux machines across the globe to ensure the reliability of Google’s content delivery and compute infrastructure. * Collaborate regularly with partner ISPs to provide remote troubleshooting and IP networking support. * Perform as part of a follow\-the\-sun operations team via a roster during Dublin core hours. * Identify and implement projects to automate and improve support, and onboard new hardware and services. * Work closely with engineering teams, hardware vendors, product managers, and partners. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: TEAM MEMBER
description: At Sprout, our Team Members are the heart of what we do. You're the face of the company — the one customers come back to see, as much as they come back for the food. From delivering warm, human interactions to helping the team keep the place humming, this is a role for someone who cares about people, pace, and good food. This is more than just a job — it’s a chance to grow, have fun, and be a BIG part of a close\-knit team that loves what they do. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time and part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.15 per hour ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Versatile Team Player – You’ll jump into different parts of the day, from helping prep fresh ingredients to setting up the service line and keeping the restaurant tidy. * Hospitality Heartbeat – Bring good energy to every interaction and make customers feel genuinely welcome and looked after. * Restaurant Ready – Keep the place sparkling clean, safe, and set up for success throughout the day. * All\-Rounder – Get hands\-on experience across our service line: till, toppings, mix – you’ll learn it all. * Detail\-Oriented – You’ll follow checklists to make sure every dish looks and tastes great. * Menu Confident – Learn our menu so you can guide customers and recommend dishes with confidence. * Safety Champion – Help the team stay on top of food safety, hygiene, and health \& safety standards. ****Who You Are**** * Hospitable – You love making people smile and creating good energy. * Polite \& Kind – Holds the door, jumps in to help, and treats people with respect. * Motivated – You care about doing a good job, no matter what the task is. * Curious – You’re eager to learn and grow. You ask questions and don’t pretend to know it all. * Positive Energy (Oomph!) – You bring great energy into the room. Excited to be here. * Team Player – You’re happiest when you’re helping others. You believe more joy comes from giving than getting. * Values\-Driven – You show up with a good attitude and live the Sprout values. ****What We’re Looking For**** * You don’t need experience for this role, we’ll teach you everything you need to know. What matters most is your attitude and willingness to learn. Here’s what we’re looking for: * Good Level of English \- You can understand instructions and communicate confidently with customers and the team. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You take feedback well and are keen to grow. * Reliable – You turn up on time and take pride in doing a good job. ****Why Work at Sprout?**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities \- we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team that genuinely care. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Tactical Communications Co-Founder / COO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description: We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups. We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1\.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B\+ companies. Our offer: * A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding. * 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis * Community: Access to the top 0\.1% of founders, peers and investors * Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals) * Distribution: Support in reaching product\-market\-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine * Funding support for securing a multi\-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise \> €2M after our Grand Pitch) One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre\-seed round by a first\-time founder, securing a €12M pre\-seed investment. **Tasks** * You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Tactical Communications * You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management * You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs * You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding * You will iterate your product with us until having reached product\-market\-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively **Requirements** * You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate * You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M\+ in revenues * You have excellent communication skills in the English language Join us and build a €1B\+ company with us!

title: Talent Acquisition Associate
description: **We’re reinventing payments.** In less than four years, Dojo disrupted the market to become the largest and most loved acquirer in the UK. Our payments infrastructure, purpose\-built for in\-person commerce, is game changing. Now, over 150,000 customers across four countries choose to transact billions with us every year. But we’re just getting started. Our people are the driving force behind our success. They are our greatest investment and our ultimate competitive advantage. We hire exceptional people and give them the autonomy, trust, and ownership to thrive. The results take care of themselves. **The Role** Reporting into our Head of Commercial, you will be supporting the Payments Consultant Recruitment function in key initiatives to ensure that we are attracting the best possible sales people in the market. This is a high volume recruitment role where you will be video interviewing, sourcing, shortlisting and telephone screening applicants, carrying out various administrative duties whilst also providing innovative thinking on how we can improve processes and continuously trying to find new ways of attracting top talent to the role. **What You Will Do…** * Shortlist high volume of applications * High volume video interviewing * Work closely with the Commercial team and provide a consultative, partnership driven approach. * Provide recommendations on new ways of sourcing candidates * Assist with various projects around candidate generation, candidate experience and process efficiencies. * Keep candidate database and spreadsheets accurate and up to date * Liaise with recruitment team to prioritise work and manage expectations * Ad hoc administrative tasks **What You Will Bring…** * An innovative and creative approach * Experience in recruiting sales people (preferably self\-employed, but not essential) * Experience in recruitment (minimum one year) * Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written * Highly organised * Proficient in Microsoft office * Self motivated to speak to a high volume of candidates * Ability and desire to work in a target driven environment * Enthusiastic and with a "can\-do" attitude **Dojo home and away** We believe our best work happens when we collaborate in\-person. These “together days” foster communication, drive innovation and spark our brightest ideas. That's why we have an office\-first culture. This means working from the office 4\+ days per week. With offices across Europe, we know a thing or two about staying dynamic. Need deep focus? Head to a quiet zone. Big ideas? Collaboration spaces have you covered. Just here for a catch\-up? Our social hubs make it easy. Do work that counts, in spaces made for you. **Question: what’s curious, relentless, and customer obsessed?** If you’re keen to know the answer, you’re a third of the way to meeting our Dojo values. **If The Following Speak To You, Let’s Talk** * You’re curious. You have a real desire to learn and create. * You’re relentless. You keep going even when it’s easier not to. * You’re customer\-obsessed. You know how important customers are to what you do. **Diversity, equity, and inclusion at Dojo** From local bakeries to well\-known eateries, Dojo payments serve over 150,000 places across the UK. And something that’s fundamental to creating relevant, innovative products at Dojo is to build teams to reflect the diversity of the businesses we serve. Our drive to improve diversity, equity, and inclusion is closely linked to helping employees thrive and innovating for better customer experiences. If you care about your work, you’re curious, and you think customer\-first, you have a place at Dojo. To make sure you’re the best you can be throughout the recruitment process, let us know if you need any extra adjustments to help you thrive. Visit dojo.careers to find out more about our benefits and what it’s like to work at Dojo, or check out our LinkedIn and Instagram pages.

title: Talent Sourcer
description: At Talent Evolution Group we are passionate about providing our clients with the best talent experience possible. We work with our clients through our managed staffing RPO (Perm) and MSP (contingent) programs as well as providing additional innovative solutions to meet their talent challenges. **Employee Experience \| Customer Experience \| Digital Transformation** We are looking for a Talent Sourcer to join our recruitment delivery team supporting our clients across the Pharmaceutical, Technology, Finance and Social Media sectors. This role is focused on ensuring we delivery on our client needs every time, finding the talent they need in the time they need it. **Key Responsibilities** * Working collaboratively with our talent engagement and onsite talent acquisition teams to define hiring needs and timelines. * Building out strategic sourcing project plans to meet requirements. * Have a deep understanding of our client’s employee value proposition and develop a go to market narrative to attract talent. * Proactively engage talent and continually build talent pools to meet our clients’ needs * Screen and assess candidates to ensure they are meeting the clients technical \& personal competencies as well as being a culture fit. * Manage the candidate through the interview and offer process. * Provide a superior candidate experience throughout the full requirement journey * Strategic sourcing strategy experience * Multitasking with clients/roles **Requirements for this role** * 2\+ years’ experience in recruitment/ talent delivery * Passionate about the talent industry and enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment * Proven track record or meeting delivery SLA’s \& KPI’s * Demonstrates the flexibility and adaptability to be successful in a changing environment * Excellent communication skills and an ability to engage and connect with people. * Enjoy working in a collaborative team environment

title: Technical Administrator - Dublin 2 - €50K
description: **Technical Administrator** * Full time office based in Dublin 2 * Opportunity to work for a leading residential developer. * Design \& Technical department **Background** Our client is a leading residential developer, specialising in housing and apartment projects. The primary objective of this role is to support the Technical team in managing and coordinating information across a range of stakeholder groups. The position will play a key role in driving consistency across the wider portfolio, while helping to implement and maintain the structures and processes required for efficient project delivery. **Package details** * Base salary up to €50,000 * Annual bonus * Pension * Medical cover * 23 days annual leave * Annual salary review * CPD funding **Responsibilities** * Monitoring document compliance (naming, format, numbering, content) * Supporting setup and management of the project document management system (DMS) * Maintaining structured file systems, workflows, and technical review processes * Ensuring consistent submission, review, approval, and distribution of project documentation * Managing RFIs, submittals, and technical queries through workflow systems * Supporting information exchange at key project stages (design reviews, tender, handover) * Coordinating technical data for sales, transactions, and purchaser requests * Assisting with BCAR documentation collation and compliance * Supporting safety file and handover documentation for project completion * Maintaining version control, audit trails, and information accuracy * Supporting reporting, presentations, and team coordination as required **Key skills required** * Experience working with CDEs or document management systems (DMS) in construction * Understanding of design processes and information management * Familiarity with construction workflows such as RFIs, submittals, design reviews, change control, and digital handover * Experience supporting construction projects

description: **Job Title:** UKI Business Consulting \- Infrastructure \& Capital Projects \- Infrastructure Controls Specialist, All Levels * **Location:** Ireland * **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship:** No * **Business Area:** UKI Business Consulting * **Contract Type:** Full\-Time – Permanent **The team and the opportunity** To continue the successful growth of our Infrastructure \& Capital Projects team within EY’s Consulting practice, we are looking for experienced and motivated Project Controls Specialists with a background in delivering complex, high‑value capital programmes. The successful candidate will be responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of project controls within the infrastructure sector, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in line with quality and governance requirements. This role requires meticulous attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate and collaborate effectively with a broad range of stakeholders. Our Infrastructure \& Capital Projects (I\&CP) team sits within the Transformation Delivery competency of EY’s Irish Consulting practice. Our I\&CP team is committed to delivering exceptional client services across a range of sectors including energy, utilities, transport, water, gas, telecoms, housing, and healthcare. Our ambition is to become the Trusted Advisor for our clients in the procurement, construction, and operational phases of major capital programmes by leveraging: * Our people and their extensive industry experience * Existing partnerships and relationships * Our global EY experience across the Infrastructure \& Capital projects lifecycle lifecycle **Your key responsibilities** To support the continued growth of EY’s I\&CP business, we are currently recruiting Project Controls Specialists with an engineering, technical, or construction‑related background. * Work directly with EY’s infrastructure clients across our focus sectors (energy, water, transport, gas, telecoms, housing, and healthcare) to develop innovative insights and solutions to meet their needs. * Develop and implement project control processes and procedures to effectively manage scope, schedule, cost, and quality. * Collaborate with project managers, engineers, contractors, and other stakeholders to establish project baselines and monitor performance against established metrics. * Create and maintain detailed project schedules, including critical path analysis and resource allocation, to optimise project timelines and identify potential risks and opportunities. * Monitor project costs and expenditures, track budget variances, and provide timely analysis and reporting to support informed decision\-making. * Identify and mitigate project risks through proactive risk assessment, analysis, and mitigation strategies. * Lead or support regular progress meetings and presentations to update project stakeholders on project status, key milestones, and performance metrics. * Utilise project management systems and tools to enhance project controls processes and enhance project visibility and transparency. * Provide mentorship and guidance to junior project controls staff, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development within the team. * Support the preparation of project proposals, bids, and cost estimates by providing accurate and reliable project controls data and analysis. * Ensure compliance with relevant industry standards, regulations, and best practices in project controls and management. **Internal Responsibilities** * Support client account management activities, including project resourcing, financial management, and quality and risk management. * Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and EY colleagues to support business develop across EY. * Communicate effectively with EY senior management and support the development, management and motivation of high\-performing teams. * Develop a strong understanding of EY service offerings and proactively identify opportunities to enhance client value. * Support team development activities, including: + Coaching, and mentoring. + Conducting performance reviews and contributing to feedback. + Supporting recruitment, retention and training initiatives. + Maintaining continuous technical and professional development. + Understanding, applying, and communicating workplace policies and procedures **To qualify for the role, you must have** * 3rd level educational qualification in Engineering, Construction, or a related field. * Minimum of 5 years of experience in project controls or project management within the infrastructure sector, with a proven track record of supporting or managing large\-scale programmes. * Ideal candidates will have:\- Experience in consulting or similar client facing role. + Multidisciplinary experience including project management and engineering feasibility and design. + Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills with a track record in managing complex programmes. + Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to analyse complex data sets and develop actionable insights. + Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with diverse stakeholders at all levels. + Demonstrated leadership capability with a proactive, delivery‑focused approach. + Knowledge of industry\-specific regulations, standards, and best practices in infrastructure development and construction. + Additional professional qualifications, such as PRINCE2® or PMP, are desirable. **What working at EY offers** We offer a competitive remuneration package. Our comprehensive Total Rewards package includes support for flexible working and career development, and with FlexEY you can select benefits that suit your needs, covering holidays, health and well\-being, insurance, savings and a wide range of discounts, offers and promotions. Plus, we offer: * Support and coaching from some of the most engaging colleagues around * Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career * The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you All our employees are given a benefits package which they can tailor to suit their individual preferences. title: Vice President, Auditor
description: At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Vice President, Auditor to join our Internal Audit team. This role is located in Dublin. In this role, you will make an impact in the following ways: * Leads or performs audits related to Finance reporting activities and works as a team member on audit assignments. * Leads risk\-focused planning, fieldwork and reporting, in accordance with Internal Audit policies, methodologies, and standards in order to identify meaningful issues, risks and other exposures. * Responsible for ensuring testing (or other tasks as applicable) is adequate and accurate to allow an evaluation of adherence to company policies and procedures, audit methodologies, and all applicable regulations. * Demonstrates skills and understanding of businesses and processes through flowcharting, work papers, analysis, issue identification and discussions with management and auditees. * Identifies inconsistencies within the control environment, regulatory requirements and best practices and able to clearly explain the audit process to clients, as well as any findings or results. * Applies advanced analytical problem\-solving skills and leads others in solving complex issues and identifying innovative solutions. To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience and have completed a recognized relevant post\-graduate qualification (e.g., CIA, CMIIA, ACA, ACCA), but not essential * 7\-10 years of total work experience preferred. * Prior experience in Audit or the relevant specialty area preferred. * Additional core skill requirements include understanding of the applied and interpretation of analytics results for Audit, critical thinking and problem solving. * Experience using AI ‑ enabled tools to support data analysis, risk identification, automation, or insight generation within a professional environment. At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: * America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 * “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: €15.41ph - Day Shift Security Officer - Dublin 9
description: 📍 **Dublin 9** 🕒 **Fully Flexible, Monday to Sunday, minimum 36 hours per week** 💰 **€15\.41 per hour plus €20\.00 night allowance and Sunday premium** Join Today and Build a Better Tomorrow! At Securitas Security Services we are united by our purpose of helping make your world a safer place, we are committed to ethics, sustainability, and compliance, our core values – Integrity, Vigilance and Helpfulness .We have created a culture where our people are empowered to be the best they can be. We believe in celebrating our differences and creating a supportive environment where all voices are heard. Our colleagues are empowered to think big and push the boundaries of innovation. As a purpose\-led organisation, we aspire to make a difference every day \- and we’re looking for the best and brightest to join our team. Career opportunities to fuel your future, together we can invest in your professional and personal growth. No matter where you are in your career, there are many training and development opportunities. Your career is in your hands, and we will help you maximize your potential! We are always looking to grow too! Join our team and use your talent and passion to help build a safer, healthier, and more efficient world. **Job Description** **Securitas Security Services are currently recruiting Customer Service Security Officers for day shifts only to be based in our large retail client site in Dublin 9\.** **Rate of pay is €15\.41 per hour plus €3\.44 on Sunday and night shift allowance of €20\.00** * Free onsite parking and subsidized canteen facilities and easily accessible by public transport\* **Main Purpose Of Position** To protect our customer’s property, people and/or assets by providing security services to a specific site in direct accordance with the sites published Assignment Instructions (AI’s) and any subsequent changes. **Expectations Of This Position** * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the training that has been agreed is necessary for the particular site. * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the tasks and duties on site as defined within the published Assignment Instructions (AI’s). * Proactively refer to and review the published Assignment Instructions, thus familiarising oneself with any changes and in order to suggest changes that can proactively improve or enhance the level of service. * Look out for, and to immediately report, any Health \& Safety hazards, or potential hazards, on site. * Stay alert to any threats or changes within the work environment in order to take the appropriate action in order to reduce the chance of disruption, loss or damage. * Use common sense and initiative by immediately involving others if a situation appears to become threatening. * Complete an accurate, detailed notebook entry as soon as possible after any incident, which can then be used as part of the Incident Report process. * Wear the uniform provided correctly at all times, ensuring all items is clean and pressed. To maintain an excellent level of personal grooming and hygiene at all times. * Maintain a helpful, interested and courteous approach to team members, customer’s staff and the general public, as applicable in order to uphold a highly professional image. **Qualifications** **Security Officer Specification:** * Good English essential * Excellent written and attention to detail skills are essential * Good level of P.C. skills * Previous experience desirable, however training will be provided * Must have excellent communication skills * Be flexible with your working hours. * PSA Licence * Strong Customer Service experience desirable **Additional Information** At Securitas, we believe in rewarding our people for their hard work. We offer a range of great benefits designed to support our people and their families. These comprehensive benefits include discounted health care scheme, company pension, bike to work scheme, access to hundreds of discounts from well\-known brands and retailers, access to hundreds of high\-quality online education courses from some of the world’s leading learning institutions, ways to save for the future, and other resources to improve health and well\-being. **Benefits** * Full\-time 36 hours a week contract of employment with the ability to work up to 48 hours per week * 24 hour EAP (Employee Assistance Programme) free confidential counselling and advice supporting our employees in a number of areas. * Employer Contribution Pension Scheme from the day you join the Company * Progression, training and development opportunities * Christmas Savings Scheme * Discounted Private Healthcare Scheme (Cash back on everyday medical costs, such as dental check\-ups, optical, GP and Emergency Department visits.) * Internal recognition schemes * Bicycle to work scheme from the day you join the Company (Save up to 52% on a bike and accessories) * Employee Refer a friend incentives * Life Assurance equal to one year’s basic pay as per Employment Regulation Order (ERO) * Overtime as per the ERO * Sick Pay Scheme as per ERO * SIPTU Representation Rights * Substantial Maternity Leave top up payment ***Securitas is an equal opportunities Employer*** **P.S.A.
